page: Microsoft Azure F16-4ams_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Famsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding an exceptionally high memory-to-core ratio of 32.0 GB per vCPU. Cache specifications include 48 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache. The CPU supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX_VNNI, and SHA_NI. This instance does not include local storage or onboard GPUs.

Benchmark data reveals strong single-core capabilities alongside moderate multi-core scaling. The server achieves top-tier performance in stress-ng single-core CPU tests, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression. Text generation speeds for small LLM models also rank in the top tier. Conversely, multi-core metrics, including stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores, fall into the average category. Memory bandwidth tests show average performance, with uncached read speeds at 55.23 GB/sec and write speeds at 34.62 GB/sec. Medium LLM model prompt processing is a notable bottleneck, registering in the bottom tier.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the F16-4ams_v7 offers high memory density relative to its compute footprint, making it advantageous for memory-intensive workloads that do not scale linearly with vCPU count. However, the lack of local storage requires external storage attachments, which may introduce latency tradeoffs. This server is suited for memory-bound applications, database operations, caching layers like Redis, and single-threaded processing tasks that benefit from high clock speeds and large memory allocations.
description: Microsoft Azure F16-4ams_v7 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server powered by a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM. Running on Microsoft Hyper-V, this instance features an exceptionally high memory-to-core ratio of 32.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or GPUs. Performance benchmarks show top-tier results in single-core CPU execution, single-threaded gzip compression, and small-model LLM text generation. However, multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall into the average tier, while medium-model LLM prompt processing is relatively weak. Qualitatively, the server provides high memory density per vCPU, offering cost-efficient resource allocation for memory-heavy workloads that do not require massive parallel compute. It is suited for memory-intensive databases, caching systems, and single-threaded processing tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F16-4ams_v7 is a memory-optimized server with 4 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.

tagline: An x86_64 memory-optimized configuration featuring high single-core clock speeds and an exceptional memory-to-vCPU ratio for memory-intensive workloads.
